feed.title: AI-bridge
feed.subtitle: Combined AI feed: Thinking Machines, Ollama, Mistral, Interconnected, AI Clock, Perplexity (blog/changelog/research/API changelog), and The Batch / DeepLearning.AI.

entry.title: I am obsessed with these two ads
entry.content: I didn’t know what a near-infrared LED mask was a week ago, and now I am obsessed with this ad from Omnilux. Because there’s a lot going on. The aesthetic of the mask itself is wild: the lines look like sinews as if the woman has been flensed But the schematic flesh is commercial! The logo on the forehead! Yet she has glossy hair and a spa outfit And is drinking a glass of white wine?? Let’s not get into the red glow through the holes in the face The ad copy is tight af, no messing around: retai

entry.title: Deep Research, command panel, and enterprise controls - June 19, 2026
entry.content: Deep Research, command panel, and enterprise controls - June 19, 2026

entry.title: Self-improving Memory for Agents
entry.content: Brain is Perplexity's self-improving memory system for Computer that learns from past work to deliver faster, more accurate results over time.

entry.title: Wet thoughts
entry.content: Wet lab. “Wet lab” was a new term to me, I had to look it up – it’s a laboratory with chemicals where there’s spill risk etc. I have a physics background myself and the closest I came to liquids was liquid nitrogen, and when you spill that it just boils furiously and goes away. Don’t do this: if you put your open hand into a dewar of liquid nitrogen, you can hold it under the liquid at -196C/-320F because the boiling on the surface of your hand makes an insulating quilt. It’s a fun game. Closing

entry.title: Introducing Perplexity Pages
entry.content: A new way to create and share knowledge on Perplexity

entry.title: Disaggregated Prefill and Decode
entry.content: Separating prefill and decode across devices speeds up LLM inference and improves performance

entry.title: Computer for Professional Finance
entry.content: Today we are launching Perplexity Computer for Professional Finance, a version of Computer built for the research, analysis, and decision workflows finance teams run every day.

entry.title: Introducing Finance Search in the Agent API
entry.content: Today we're launching Finance Search in the Perplexity Agent API. In a single tool call, Finance Search combines licensed financial datasets, real-time market data, and cited web sources.

entry.title: How We Built Security Into Computer
entry.content: Perplexity Computer is built directly on Perplexity's existing infrastructure, which has completed its 2026 SOC 2 Type II attestation, and inherits Perplexity’s enterprise security features such as SAML SSO, audit logs, and granular administrative controls.

entry.title: How Computer is Reshaping Knowledge Work
entry.content: This week, in collaboration with researchers at Harvard Business School, we published a new study looking at how people actually use Perplexity Computer in the real world.

entry.title: Deep Research, now in Computer
entry.content: Deep Research in Computer answers hard questions with trusted data, then delivers reports, dashboards, and decks.

entry.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
entry.content: Ollama's MLX engine has been updated to deliver its highest performance on Apple Silicon yet. Models output higher quality responses, respond faster, and use less memory.

entry.title: Bringing European AI Models to Global Audiences
entry.content: Perplexity will integrate sovereign European AI models into our answer engine, bringing locally-optimized, culturally-aware AI to users worldwide while supporting Europe's digital sovereignty initiatives.

entry.title: Book a Table with Perplexity and OpenTable
entry.content: Book your next dining experience effortlessly with Perplexity’s new OpenTable partnership.

entry.title: How We Built Security Into Comet From Day One
entry.content: Explore Perplexity's blog for articles, announcements, product updates, and tips to optimize your experience. Stay informed and make the most of Perplexity.

entry.title: Hosting Qwen on Blackwell
entry.content: Perplexity Research advances our mission to transform how we navigate the internet and the wider world through frontier research in search, reasoning, agents, and systems.

entry.title: Query-Aware Context Compression for Better Snippets
entry.content: Improving the quality-efficiency frontier of model context through query-aware context compression models.

entry.title: Improving Unigram Tokenizer CPU Performance
entry.content: We reimplemented our Unigram tokenizer from scratch as a focused performance project.

entry.title: Rethinking Search as Code Generation
entry.content: Evolving search from monolithic services to programmable primitives for the era of agent harnesses.

entry.title: How AI Agents Reshape Knowledge Work
entry.content: Computer raises task autonomy, lowers cost, and widens the scope of work users take on.

entry.title: Self-driving legs to walk me to the office
entry.content: When we end up voluntarily ceding control of our own body parts to AI (for example so we can automatically walk around the grocery story while simultaneously catching up on boxsets on a VR headset) I wonder what that will feel like. The underlying tech is currently in research phase. What I mean is that humanoid robots are coming (as previously discussed). And while robots are a mechanical challenge and a software challenge, some people are asking: what if you took that software part and pointed

entry.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
entry.content: Ollama 0.30 is now available with improved performance and GGUF model compatibility through llama.cpp. This augments Ollama's MLX engine on Apple silicon, bringing support to more models on a wider range of hardware.

entry.title: Introducing PPLX Online LLMs
entry.content: The first-of-its-kind Online LLM API

entry.title: Introducing the Perplexity Publishers’ Program
entry.content: From day one, we’ve included citations in each answer, ensuring publishers receive proper credit and building user trust.

entry.title: Efficient and Portable Mixture-of-Experts Communication
entry.content: An overview of portable Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) communication, focusing on optimizing GPU parallelism and reducing latency in large-scale AI models

entry.title: Introducing Comet: Browse at the speed of thought
entry.content: Comet is a web browser built for today’s internet. In the last 30 years, the internet has evolved from something we simply “browse” or “search.” The internet is where we live, work, and connect.

entry.title: Secure Credentials on Comet with 1Password
entry.content: Perplexity Partners with 1Password in First AI Browser Partnership for Password Security

entry.title: The New Comet Assistant
entry.content: Explore Perplexity's blog for articles, announcements, product updates, and tips to optimize your experience. Stay informed and make the most of Perplexity.

entry.title: Finish Your Annual Plan Faster With Perplexity
entry.content: Use Perplexity to turn messy annual planning into a fast, focused system your team can actually execute.

entry.title: Introducing the Perplexity Health Advisory Board
entry.content: The Perplexity Health Advisory Board is a small group of practicing physicians, researchers, and health technology leaders who will help ensure that our future product decisions, content quality, patient safety, and clinical workflows reflect evidence-based medicine.

entry.title: Computer at Work
entry.content: Last month, we launched Computer for Enterprise. At Ask NYC 2026, our inaugural enterprise conference, we shared what's next: more integrations, data connectors, and workflow tools built around how enterprise teams actually work

entry.title: Computer comes to Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and Outlook
entry.content: Perplexity Computer runs inside Microsoft 365 apps. Draft docs, build models, generate decks, and compose email in-app.

entry.title: Perplexity Pro is coming to all SK Telecom users
entry.content: We’re teaming up with the global telecommunications company and largest mobile network in Korea to expand access to 32.5 million new users.

entry.title: Meet our First Channel Partners & Data Integrators
entry.content: Expanding our footprint for Enterprise Pro by partnering with industry leaders

entry.title: Shop like a Pro
entry.content: Perplexity’s new AI-powered shopping assistant

entry.title: Perplexity’s Fall 2024 Campus Strategist Program
entry.content: Here’s how our 2024 Campus Strategists brought AI search to life through hackathons, events, and innovative outreach.

entry.title: Perplexity Expands Partnership with SoftBank to Launch Enterprise Pro Japan
entry.content: Perplexity partners with SoftBank to bring Enterprise Pro to Japanese corporations.

entry.title: Perplexity x Lewis Hamilton
entry.content: Today we’re announcing a global partnership between Perplexity and Lewis Hamilton - a collaboration that brings together the relentless pursuit of speed, precision, and curiosity.

entry.title: Perplexity x Theo Von’s This Past Weekend
entry.content: Today we’re announcing a new partnership with The Roost Podcast Network and Theo Von’s hit show, “This Past Weekend.” Our partnership pairs one of the world’s most famously inquisitive minds with the AI-powered answer engine built for people who are curious.

entry.title: Introducing Perplexity for Government
entry.content: An initiative that centralizes and expands our efforts to serve those who serve America

entry.title: A Personal Assistant for Your Inbox
entry.content: Today we're launching Perplexity Email Assistant, exclusive for Perplexity Max subscribers. Email Assistant brings a powerful personal assistant to your email, transforming your inbox to action.

entry.title: Shopping That Puts You First
entry.content: Explore Perplexity's blog for articles, announcements, product updates, and tips to optimize your experience. Stay informed and make the most of Perplexity.

entry.title: Introducing Perplexity Computer
entry.content: Perplexity Computer unifies every current AI capability into a single system. Computer is a general-purpose digital worker that operates the same interfaces you do.

entry.title: Sandbox API: Isolated Code Execution for AI Agents
entry.content: Explore Perplexity's blog for articles, announcements, product updates, and tips to optimize your experience. Stay informed and make the most of Perplexity.

entry.title: Perplexity APIs Power AI Browsing on Samsung Devices
entry.content: This week, Samsung launched Browsing Assist, a conversational AI assistant built directly into Samsung Browser on Galaxy Android devices and Windows PC, reaching up to 1 billion Samsung devices. Perplexity built the APIs that power it.

entry.title: Introducing Computer for Taxes
entry.content: Computer for Taxes answers U.S. tax questions fast and accurately, drafting returns and reviewing for compliance

entry.title: Personal Computer is Coming to Windows
entry.content: Computer for Windows acts on local files, orchestrates Microsoft apps, and works across the web.

entry.title: Personal Computer Is Here
entry.content: Personal Computer brings the multi-model orchestration of Computer to your machine, working across files, apps, and the web.

entry.title: Perplexity raises Series A funding round
entry.content: Announcing Perplexity's Series A Funding Round and Mobile App Launch

entry.title: Introducing pplx-api
entry.content: Perplexity Lab's fast and efficient API for open-source LLMs

entry.title: Turbocharging Llama 2 70B with NVIDIA H100
entry.content: The journey of accelerated LLM inference

entry.title: Perplexity raises Series B funding round
entry.content: Announcing Perplexity's Series B Funding Round

entry.title: Arc x Perplexity
entry.content: A new internet deserves a new window.

entry.title: Perplexity Partners with ElevenLabs to launch 'Discover Daily' Podcast
entry.content: Bringing cutting-edge knowledge to your ears

entry.title: Perplexity launches Enterprise Pro
entry.content: Announces $62.7M in funding and partnerships with SoftBank + Deutsche Telekom

entry.title: Bringing Perplexity to education and not-for-profits
entry.content: Perplexity Enterprise Pro, with special rates for philanthropic organizations, public servants, and schools

entry.title: Pro Search: Upgraded for more advanced problem-solving
entry.content: Research shapes our daily lives. We use it to make informed decisions and solve problems—to innovate, learn, and grow.

entry.title: Perplexity collaborates with Amazon Web Services to launch Enterprise Pro
entry.content: We’re taking another major step in giving organizations the ability to leverage AI-powered tools for greater efficiency and productivity.

entry.title: Eligible Uber One members can now unlock a complimentary full year of Perplexity Pro
entry.content: Uber One members can now save even more time with perks like Pro Search

entry.title: Perplexity makes gift to Northwestern Medill to research AI and journalism
entry.content: Our $250K gift to Northwestern Medill supports cutting-edge research on AI's role in journalism, exploring how our technology can enhance reporting, fact-checking, and news consumption in the digital age.

entry.title: Redeem a free year of Perplexity Pro through Xfinity Rewards
entry.content: Perplexity is thrilled to offer Perplexity Pro—our premium consumer subscription—to Xfinity customers through its customer loyalty program

entry.title: Getting started with Perplexity
entry.content: Perplexity is an AI search engine that delivers fast, clear answers with real-time insights. Get reliable information without sifting through endless links.

entry.title: Introducing Internal Knowledge Search and Spaces
entry.content: Enhanced file analysis and collaboration features on Perplexity

entry.title: A student's guide to using Perplexity Spaces
entry.content: Explore Perplexity's blog for articles, announcements, product updates, and tips to optimize your experience. Stay informed and make the most of Perplexity.

entry.title: About the Dow Jones lawsuit
entry.content: About the Dow Jones lawsuit

entry.title: Introducing the Election Information Hub
entry.content: Understand key issues, vote intelligently, and track election results all on Perplexity.

entry.title: Why we’re experimenting with advertising
entry.content: Users come to Perplexity for a more efficient, uncluttered, and unbiased search experience, and that isn’t changing.

entry.title: Research nonprofits with Charity Navigator on Perplexity
entry.content: Today is Giving Tuesday, and we're making it easier than ever to research and donate to U.S. nonprofits thanks to a new collaboration with Charity Navigator.

entry.title: Perplexity Expands Publisher Program with 15 New Media Partners
entry.content: Perplexity added major media companies to its publisher revenue-sharing program, including the Los Angeles Times, The Independent, and ADWEEK12. The expansion brings diverse content partners across multiple regions and specialties, from local news to specialized publications

entry.title: Welcoming Carbon to the Perplexity Team
entry.content: We're excited to announce that we've acquired Carbon, a retrieval engine that connects external data sources to large language models.

entry.title: Introducing the Sonar Pro API by Perplexity
entry.content: Explore Perplexity's blog for articles, announcements, product updates, and tips to optimize your experience. Stay informed and make the most of Perplexity.

entry.title: High-Performance GPU Memory Transfer on AWS Sagemaker Hyperpod
entry.content: Journey to 3200 Gbps

entry.title: Meet New Sonar
entry.content: A Blazing Fast Model Optimized for Perplexity Search

entry.title: Introducing Perplexity Deep Research
entry.content: Deep Research accelerates question answering by completing in 2-4 minutes what would take a human expert many hours.

entry.title: Improved Sonar Models: Industry Leading Performance at Lower Costs
entry.content: New tiered search modes balance cost and performance, delivering superior results at the best price

entry.title: Rebuilding TikTok in America
entry.content: A Vision for Rebuilding TikTok in America

entry.title: Perplexity Sonar Dominates New Search Arena Evaluation
entry.content: Perplexity Sonar models claim top spots in search evaluation

entry.title: How Perplexity Enterprise Pro Keeps Your Data Secure
entry.content: With advanced privacy protections, admin controls, and compliance-ready infrastructure, Enterprise Pro provides your team with a powerful answer engine and deep research tools for the web and internal files while keeping your data private and secure.

entry.title: Lower Latency and Higher Throughput with Multi-node DeepSeek Deployment
entry.content: Multi-GPU deployment boosts MoE model performance on both speed and scale fronts simultaneously

entry.title: Choice is the Remedy
entry.content: At Perplexity, we don’t see ourselves as a competitor to Google. We’re building something different. We're giving people a choice: search that answers, assistants that work. AI that's intelligent, accurate, and trustworthy.

entry.title: Announcing Our Global Partnership with Motorola
entry.content: We're excited to announce our global partnership with Motorola. Starting with the new generation of Motorola devices, Perplexity will be pre-installed on millions of smartphones worldwide, giving Motorola users direct access to our answer engine and assistant.

entry.title: RL Training For Math Reasoning
entry.content: Boosting Math Reasoning in LLMs with Reinforcement Learning and Smart Data Mixing

entry.title: Perplexity Partners with Wiley to Power Educational AI Search
entry.content: Perplexity is partnering with Wiley, one of the world’s largest publishers and trusted leader in research and learning to combine our answer engine with their extensive library of scientific, technical and medical learning materials.

entry.title: Perplexity and SAP: Turbocharging Joule with Real-Time Answers for Every Enterprise
entry.content: Perplexity has teamed up with SAP to integrate its AI-powered answer engine into SAP Joule, providing businesses with instant, context-driven insights to enhance decision-making with real-time, secure data.

entry.title: More Value In Every Answer: New Benefits For Every Level of Perplexity User
entry.content: Perplexity Pro Perks gives Pro users special deals and offers from top brands in travel, health, finance, and more. These offers are built right into Perplexity, so Pro members can easily access them as part of their subscription.

entry.title: Introducing Perplexity Labs
entry.content: Perplexity Labs are a way to bring your projects to life faster than ever before

entry.title: Answers for Every Investor
entry.content: All investors share one thing: curiosity.

entry.title: Accelerating Sonar Through Speculation
entry.content: Speculative decoding accelerates Sonar LLMs via draft model verification

entry.title: Introducing Perplexity Max
entry.content: Our most advanced subscription tier yet, built for those who demand limitless AI productivity and immediate access to the newest products and features from Perplexity.

entry.title: Welcoming Gannett to the Perplexity Publisher Program
entry.content: Gannett (USA TODAY + 200 local outlets) licenses content to Perplexity, enriching its AI search and Comet browser with trusted journalism while adopting Sonar API and Enterprise Pro.

entry.title: Agents or Bots? Making Sense of AI on the Open Web
entry.content: Modern AI assistants work fundamentally differently from traditional web crawling.

entry.title: GPT-OSS on Day 0
entry.content: Day‑0 support for GPT‑OSS on H200 by adapting ROSE with FP8, sink attention, and MoE optimizations

entry.title: The Intelligent Business: Introducing Comet for Enterprise Pro
entry.content: Yesterday we launched Comet to all US Perplexity Pro subscribers. Today we are shipping Comet to all Perplexity Enterprise Pro users.

entry.title: Introducing Comet Plus
entry.content: Comet Plus is a new subscription that gives Perplexity users access to premium content from a group of trusted publishers and journalists.

entry.title: Power your organization’s full potential
entry.content: Today, we're excited to announce Perplexity Enterprise Max, our most advanced tier that combines all the advanced capabilities of Max with the enterprise-grade security and controls that organizations demand.

entry.title: Introducing the Perplexity Search API
entry.content: Providing access to the same global-scale infrastructure that powers Perplexity’s public answer engine

entry.title: Announcing Comet Plus Launch Partners
entry.content: Delivering premium journalism to a more curious, empowered internet while ensuring that publishers benefit too

entry.title: The Internet is Better on Comet
entry.content: Today we are releasing the Comet browser to the world, for free.

entry.title: Mitigating Prompt Injection in Comet
entry.content: Explore Perplexity's blog for articles, announcements, product updates, and tips to optimize your experience. Stay informed and make the most of Perplexity.

entry.title: AI at work: getting more done with less hype
entry.content: Our view of AI is pretty simple: the most powerful application of AI is for it to be useful to people.

entry.title: Introducing Perplexity Patents: AI-Powered Patent Search for Everyone
entry.content: Explore Perplexity's blog for articles, announcements, product updates, and tips to optimize your experience. Stay informed and make the most of Perplexity.

entry.title: Introducing Privacy Snapshot
entry.content: Comet is the world’s first browser built from the ground up to be an AI assistant. Knowing we were building one of the first AI browsers and powerful personal assistants, we designed Comet to have privacy and security at the core.

entry.title: Bullying is Not Innovation
entry.content: The point of technology is to make life better for people. We call it innovation, but it’s just the constant process of asking how to make things better. Bullying, on the other hand, is when large corporations use legal threats and intimidation to block innovation and make life worse for people.

entry.title: Comet Assistant puts you in control
entry.content: Explore Perplexity's blog for articles, announcements, product updates, and tips to optimize your experience. Stay informed and make the most of Perplexity.

entry.title: Announcing our Partnership with the United States Government
entry.content: Explore Perplexity's blog for articles, announcements, product updates, and tips to optimize your experience. Stay informed and make the most of Perplexity.

entry.title: Comet for Android is Here
entry.content: Explore Perplexity's blog for articles, announcements, product updates, and tips to optimize your experience. Stay informed and make the most of Perplexity.

entry.title: Introducing AI assistants with memory
entry.content: With our upgraded personalization and memory, Perplexity remembers key details across conversations so future answers are more personalized and efficient.

entry.title: Building Safer AI Browsers with BrowseSafe
entry.content: Explore Perplexity's blog for articles, announcements, product updates, and tips to optimize your experience. Stay informed and make the most of Perplexity.

entry.title: Perplexity x Cristiano Ronaldo
entry.content: Today we are announcing that Perplexity has entered a global partnership with Cristiano Ronaldo.

entry.title: How People Use AI Agents
entry.content: Explore Perplexity's blog for articles, announcements, product updates, and tips to optimize your experience. Stay informed and make the most of Perplexity.

entry.title: Introducing Perplexity for Public Safety Organizations
entry.content: Perplexity is now the first frontier AI company to offer Enterprise Pro specifically to law enforcement and public safety agencies.

entry.title: Happy 25th Birthday, Wikipedia
entry.content: Twenty-five years ago today, Wikipedia launched with a simple idea: that wisdom is everywhere, and technology should help more people access it.

entry.title: Inside the Rise of Enterprise AI Model-Switching
entry.content: Enterprise usage data shows employees are now treating models as a menu of options, not a single default tool.

entry.title: Introducing Model Council
entry.content: Today we are launching Model Council, a multi-model research feature that brings several models together for one answer.

entry.title: The AI is the Computer
entry.content: Explore Perplexity's blog for articles, announcements, product updates, and tips to optimize your experience. Stay informed and make the most of Perplexity.

entry.title: Everything is Computer
entry.content: Today we are announcing Personal Computer.  Personal Computer runs on a dedicated Mac mini that can run 24/7, connected to your local apps and Perplexity’s secure servers.

entry.title: Agent API: A Managed Runtime for Agentic Workflows
entry.content: Today we are releasing the Perplexity Agent API, a managed runtime for building agentic workflows with integrated search, tool execution, and multi-model orchestration.

entry.title: Search API: Better Extraction, Dynamic Benchmarks
entry.content: Our Search API snippet quality has been optimized along two dimensions: relevance and size.

entry.title: Computer for Enterprise
entry.content: The enterprise-ready version of Computer orchestrates 20 advanced models to execute multi-step projects for businesses.

entry.title: Announcing Premium Sources
entry.content: Computer can now access select Statista, CB Insights, and PitchBook data directly as part of your research workflow.

entry.title: Perplexity Joins the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition
entry.content: The NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition is a collaboration of AI labs working together to advance open models. Today we are announcing Perplexity is a founding member of this important initiative.

entry.title: Comet Enterprise is here
entry.content: Comet Enterprise is a secure, team-ready AI browser that boosts productivity with admin controls and CrowdStrike protection.

entry.title: Comet is Now Available on iOS
entry.content: Comet for iOS brings AI-powered browsing, instant answers, and seamless research to the iPhone.

entry.title: Introducing Perplexity Health
entry.content: Today we are launching Perplexity Health, a suite of connectors to your personal health data that allows for powerful personal uses of Perplexity Computer, providing accurate health information for the most important questions.

entry.title: Announcing the Secure Intelligence Institute
entry.content: Today we're launching the Secure Intelligence Institute (SII). The Secure Intelligence Institute is Perplexity's flagship research center for advancing security, privacy, and trust in frontier AI.

entry.title: Computer in Slack: From Shared Context to Finished Work
entry.content: Like many breakthrough technologies, Perplexity Computer began as an internal tool at Perplexity. Computer was born in the place where we do most of our work: Slack.

entry.title: Computer is Now Your Personal CFO
entry.content: Perplexity links to accounts through Plaid for personalized debt plans, retirement dashboards, and cash flow forecasts.

entry.title: How Perplexity Builds Accuracy into Frontier AI
entry.content: Our central focus has always been to build accuracy into frontier AI. For the first time, we’re sharing some of the technology behind how we do that.

entry.title: Computer in Teams is here
entry.content: Perplexity brings AI orchestration to Microsoft Teams for analysis, creation, and workflows where work happens

entry.title: Announcing Premium Health Sources
entry.content: Today we are launching Premium Health Sources. This continues our investment in premium sources and will allow Perplexity to draw from the same clinical references used by physicians and researchers.

entry.title: Personal Computer is Available to All Mac Users
entry.content: Personal Computer is now available in the new Perplexity Mac app.

entry.title: Computer brings data science to every team
entry.content: Make data accessible with Computer. Query Snowflake in plain language, uncover insights, and drive faster decisions.

entry.title: Perplexity Is Open-Sourcing Bumblebee
entry.content: Today we are open-sourcing one of the internal tools we use to protect the developer systems behind Perplexity, Comet, and Computer.

entry.title: The Data Center Moves to Your Machine
entry.content: Today we announced the next step for Personal Computer: the first hybrid local-server inference orchestrator.

entry.title: Perplexity Computer for Growing Businesses
entry.content: Today Perplexity is announcing the Main Street AI Accelerator, a $25 million Computer credits program, in collaboration with the Small Business Administration.

entry.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
entry.content: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra is built for high-throughput reasoning and long-running agent workflows.

entry.title: How global logistics got me over my fear of personal agents
entry.content: Ok a bunch of friends have their own personal AI agent using OpenClaw or Hermes or whatever. Confession time: I don’t. I haven’t found I need one. I’m a heavy user of agents for coding, sure, and a few isolated tasks. I haven’t found I need one digging through my notes or sending emails for me. But really because of two fears: Data security. I’m cautious of something that can non-deterministically execute and get tricked into emailing confidential info or accidentally delete old photos without m

entry.title: Computer in Microsoft 365, context panel, and analytics dashboards - May 29, 2026
entry.content: Computer in Microsoft 365, context panel, and analytics dashboards - May 29, 2026

entry.title: AI Now Summit 2026
entry.content: Innovations for global enterprises solving the world’s hardest problems.

entry.title: Vibe gets to work.
entry.content: The unified agent for long-horizon productivity and coding, launching with Work and Code modes. Plus, a new Vibe VS Code extension.

entry.title: Introducing Search Toolkit
entry.content: Search Toolkit is a composable framework for building production search pipelines for AI applications.

entry.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
entry.content: OpenJarvis v1.0 is now available: an open-source framework for building personal AI agents that run on your own hardware, with Ollama support built-in.

entry.title: Introducing physics AI at Mistral: the foundation for engineering acceleration.
entry.content: A new class of AI models that predict the behavior of physical systems, powering the engineers and hardware products of tomorrow.

entry.title: Physics AI research that’s shaping the industry.
entry.content: Published breakthroughs pushing the state of the art.

entry.title: Emmi joins Mistral to accelerate the AI-native industry
entry.content: Emmi joins Mistral to accelerate the AI-native industry

entry.title: Remote agents in Vibe. Powered by Mistral Medium 3.5.
entry.content: Introducing Mistral Medium 3.5, remote coding agents in Vibe, plus new Work mode in Le Chat for complex tasks.

entry.title: Connect the dots: Build with built-in and custom MCPs in Studio
entry.content: Connect enterprise data to your AI applications with reusable connectors, direct tool calling, and human-in-the-loop approval controls.

entry.title: Resident: vibe coding firmware (our new sandbox library for ESP32 devices)
entry.content: We’re open sourcing Resident, our library for running AI-authored code on microcontrollers – with no compile step and no firmware flashing. It’s our twist on vibe coding firmware, built for instantly loading new device functionality coded by end users. It’s aimed at device developers, like us. We use Resident in all our work. (We = Inanimate.) Resident gives you a code sandbox on ESP32 devices and a driver API to provide hardware control and events. So an end user can push an app over Wi-Fi that

entry.title: Filtered for bad addresses and good emotions
entry.content: 1. I love this terrible fake address generator: We have spent 3 years collecting data from every country to generate this enormous database. …it promises. These are not English addresses: "2 Scott Common Jenniferstad S60 2PT" "4 Matilda Via North Emilychester M20 1BT" "Flat 48v Harrison Motorway Lake Gary EH4 5LQ" Good story: Inside Number 0 , John Finnemore. 2. Kentucky Superstitions (1920) by Daniel Lindsey Thomas and Lucy Blayney Thomas: Some household and domestic life superstitions, from pa

entry.title: Personal Computer on Mac for all - May 11, 2026
entry.content: Personal Computer on Mac for all - May 11, 2026

entry.title: Interaction Models: A Scalable Approach to Human-AI Collaboration
entry.content: Today, we’re announcing a research preview of interaction models: models that handle interaction natively rather than through external scaffolding. We think interactivity should scale alongside intelligence; the way we work with AI should not be treated as an afterthought. Interaction models let people collaborate with AI the way we naturally collaborate with each other—they continuously take in audio, video, and text, and think, respond, and act in real time. We train an interaction model from

entry.title: I want my MTV
entry.content: You know when I’m home on my own I don’t watch much TV like actual TV, but I do watch music videos on YouTube on the TV. Sometimes new music but often ones I know really well. Music videos are such a vibe. And it’s social too? I’ve spent many evenings with friends that just turn into swapping music vids on the big screen. I seem to listen to music only while doing something else, e.g.: while watching music videos, as above while running while playing Grand Theft Auto and actually that was the ma

entry.title: May 2026
entry.content: Finance Search: Now Available The finance_search tool is now available in the Agent API. Pull structured financial and market data — quotes, financials, earnings, analyst estimates, segment KPIs, ETF constituents, and more — for public companies and instruments. The model decides which fields to fetch based on your prompt, so a single call can return valuation, earnings, and context together. Highlights: Quotes and pricing : Near-real-time prices, OHLCV ranges, pre-market and after-hours data Fi

entry.title: Improved Computer Models and Enterprise Updates - May 4, 2026
entry.content: Improved Computer Models and Enterprise Updates - May 4, 2026

entry.title: We need RSS for sharing abundant vibe-coded apps
entry.content: Now we have abundant apps, we’ve hit a ceiling in good ways to track and share them. For example, Simon Willison’s personal tools : "Miscellaneous HTML+JavaScript tools built mostly with the help of LLMs." Willison lists 80+ useful tools and the search box can see 205. That’s a lot! He has hyper-specific tools like one to track daylight savings changes in California, personal software to help build his newsletter from his blog, and a tool to edit using Strunk & White’s principle to omit needless

entry.title: Workflows for work that runs the business
entry.content: Workflows is now in public preview.

entry.title: The Wind in the Willows and reading out loud
entry.content: The Wind in the Willows right? Kenneth Grahame, 1908. We all know the children’s story inside-out. Mole and Ratty and gruff Badger and conceited Mr Toad with his motorcars and all their adventures. I picked up the book as an adult - I can’t remember why - and it wasn’t what I expected. This week I have been reading it again and it is again astonishing. I mean… let me share some of the prose with you. This is when Mole encounters the river for the first time. Green turf sloped down to either edge

entry.title: Courier: real-time messaging for ESP32 with batteries included (new library)
entry.content: I hack on hardware a whole bunch, at Inanimate and at home. AI is in the cloud. Interaction is in my room and in my hands. The job always begins by wiring together those two ends. So the second thing I do, every single time, is bring up real-time messaging using JSON over websockets so I can connect my new device to a server, and have it emit events and listen for commands. (The first thing I do is bring up the hardware and get basic blinkenlights .) I want my on-device websockets client to have

entry.title: Personal Computer on Mac Launch and Computer Updates - April 17, 2026
entry.content: Personal Computer on Mac Launch and Computer Updates - April 17, 2026

entry.title: March 2026
entry.content: Agent API: New Third-Party Models The Agent API now supports additional third-party models including GPT-5.4, NVIDIA Nemotron, Claude Sonnet 4.6, and Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview — giving you more flexibility for tool-calling, structured outputs, and model fallback chains. Model Deprecations: Gemini 2.5 Flash & Gemini 2.5 Pro As of March 20, 2026, google/gemini-2.5-flash has been deprecated and removed from the API. google/gemini-2.5-pro followed on April 1, along with google/gemini-3-pro-preview . If

entry.title: April 2026
entry.content: Agent API: New Third-Party Models The Agent API now supports Claude Opus 4.7, GPT-5.5, and Grok 4.20 Reasoning — extending model choice for tool-calling, structured outputs, and fallback chains. See the full list in the Agent API Models reference . API Key Management: Security Upgrade We've upgraded API key management with a one-time reveal model: full token values are now returned only at the moment of creation and cannot be retrieved again from the console or any endpoint. This significantly r

entry.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
entry.content: Today, we're previewing the fastest way to run Ollama on Apple silicon, powered by MLX, Apple's machine learning framework.

entry.title: Comet iOS Launch and Computer Updates - March 27, 2026
entry.content: Comet iOS Launch and Computer Updates - March 27, 2026

entry.title: Speaking of Voxtral
entry.content: Voxtral TTS: A frontier, open-weights text-to-speech model that’s fast, instantly adaptable, and produces lifelike speech for voice agents.

entry.title: Introducing Forge
entry.content: Today, we’re introducing Forge, a system for enterprises to build frontier-grade AI models grounded in their proprietary knowledge.

entry.title: Introducing Mistral Small 4
entry.content: Introducing Mistral Small 4

entry.title: Mistral AI partners with NVIDIA to accelerate open frontier models
entry.content: Mistral AI partners with NVIDIA to accelerate open frontier models

entry.title: Leanstral: Open-Source foundation for trustworthy vibe-coding
entry.content: Leanstral: Open-Source foundation for trustworthy vibe-coding

entry.title: What We Shipped - March 13, 2026
entry.content: What We Shipped - March 13, 2026

entry.title: Rails testing on autopilot: Building an agent that writes what developers won't
entry.content: Rails testing on autopilot: Building an agent that writes what developers won't

entry.title: February 2026
entry.content: Agent API: Now Available We're excited to announce the general availability of the Agent API ! Build autonomous agents with production-ready guidance on model behavior, output controls, and OpenAI-compatibility patterns to seamlessly integrate with your existing systems. Start here: Agent API Quickstart Embeddings API: Now Available We're thrilled to launch the Embeddings API with comprehensive guides for standard and contextualized embeddings, plus best practices for semantic search and retriev

entry.title: The simplest and fastest way to setup OpenClaw
entry.content: Setup OpenClaw in under two minutes with a single Ollama command.

entry.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
entry.content: Ollama now supports subagents and web search in Claude Code.

entry.title: Voxtral transcribes at the speed of sound.
entry.content: Voxtral transcribes at the speed of sound.

entry.title: OpenClaw
entry.content: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant that connects your messaging apps to local AI coding agents, all running on your own device.

entry.title: Terminally online Mistral Vibe.
entry.content: Terminally online Mistral Vibe.

entry.title: ollama launch
entry.content: ollama launch is a new command which sets up and runs coding tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex with local or cloud models. No environment variables or config files needed.

entry.title: Heaps do lie: debugging a memory leak in vLLM.
entry.content: Heaps do lie: debugging a memory leak in vLLM.

entry.title: Image generation (experimental)
entry.content: Generate images locally with Ollama on macOS. Windows and Linux support coming soon.

entry.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
entry.content: Ollama is now compatible with the Anthropic Messages API, making it possible to use tools like Claude Code with open models.

entry.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
entry.content: Open models can be used with OpenAI's Codex CLI through Ollama. Codex can read, modify, and execute code in your working directory using models such as gpt-oss:20b, gpt-oss:120b, or other open-weight alternatives.

entry.title: December 2025
entry.content: Model Deprecation: sonar-reasoning Removed As of December 15, 2025, the sonar-reasoning model has been deprecated and removed from the API. If you were using this model, we recommend migrating to sonar-reasoning-pro for enhanced multi-step reasoning capabilities with web search. New: Media Classifier for Intelligent Visual Content We're excited to introduce the Media Classifier — an intelligent system that automatically detects when your queries would benefit from visual content and includes rel

entry.title: October 2025
entry.content: Official Perplexity SDKs We're thrilled to announce the official Perplexity SDKs for Python and Typescript! These SDKs provide convenient, type-safe access to all Perplexity APIs with both synchronous and asynchronous clients. Installation: Features: Full type definitions for all request parameters and response fields Support for Sonar and Search APIs Streaming support with async iterators Automatic environment variable handling for API keys Get started with our SDK Quickstart Guide and explore

entry.title: November 2025
entry.content: Pro Search: Now Generally Available We're excited to announce the general availability of Pro Search for Sonar Pro! Pro Search enhances your queries with automated tool usage, enabling multi-step reasoning through intelligent tool orchestration. Key capabilities: Multi-step reasoning : The model automatically performs multiple web searches and fetches URL content to answer complex queries Real-time thought streaming : Watch the model's reasoning process as it works through your question Automati

entry.title: Introducing Mistral OCR 3
entry.content: Introducing Mistral OCR 3

entry.title: Introducing: Devstral 2 and Mistral Vibe CLI.
entry.content: Introducing: Devstral 2 and Mistral Vibe CLI.

entry.title: Introducing Mistral 3
entry.content: Introducing Mistral 3

entry.title: Mistral AI - KI für Deutschland
entry.content: Mistral AI - KI für Deutschland

entry.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
entry.content: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools) to bring the latest gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models to users for safety classification tasks. gpt-oss-safeguard models are available in two sizes: 20B and 120B, and are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license.

entry.title: MiniMax M2
entry.content: MiniMax M2 is now available on Ollama's cloud. It's a model built for coding and agentic workflows.

entry.title: On-Policy Distillation
entry.content: LLMs are capable of expert performance in focused domains, a result of several capabilities stacked together: perception of input, knowledge retrieval, plan selection, and reliable execution. This requires a stack of training approaches, which we can divide into three broad stages: Pre-training teaches general capacities such as language use, broad reasoning, and world knowledge. Mid-training imparts domain knowledge, such as code, medical databases, or internal company documents. Post-training

entry.title: Introducing Mistral AI Studio.
entry.content: Introducing Mistral AI Studio.

entry.title: NVIDIA DGX Spark performance
entry.content: We ran performance tests on release day firmware and an updated Ollama version to see how Ollama performs.

entry.title: New coding models & integrations
entry.content: GLM-4.6 and Qwen3-coder-480B are available on Ollama’s cloud service with easy integrations to the tools you are familiar with. Qwen3-Coder-30B has been updated for faster, more reliable tool calling in Ollama’s new engine.

entry.title: Qwen3-VL
entry.content: Ollama now supports Alibaba's Qwen3-VL.

entry.title: NVIDIA DGX Spark
entry.content: The latest NVIDIA DGX Spark is here! Ollama has partnered with NVIDIA to ensure it runs fast and efficiently out-of-the-box.

entry.title: LoRA Without Regret
entry.content: Today’s leading language models contain upwards of a trillion parameters, pretrained on tens of trillions of tokens. Base model performance keeps improving with scale, as these trillions are necessary for learning and representing all the patterns in written-down human knowledge. In contrast, post-training involves smaller datasets and generally focuses on narrower domains of knowledge and ranges of behavior. It seems wasteful to use a terabit of weights to represent updates from a gigabit or me

entry.title: Modular Manifolds
entry.content: When we train large neural networks, we need to keep them healthy. We do not want the tensors in the network—either the weights, activations or gradients—to grow too large or too small. Very small and very large tensors cause a variety of problems not just limited to numerical underflow and overflow. For example, weight matrices changing size during training makes it harder to design training algorithms—since the relative size of updates to weights has a significant impact on the speed of learni

entry.title: September 2025
entry.content: New: File Attachments Support You can now upload and analyze documents in multiple formats using Sonar models! This powerful new feature supports PDF, DOC, DOCX, TXT, and RTF files, allowing you to ask questions, extract information, and get summaries from your documents. Key capabilities: Document Analysis : Ask questions about document content and get detailed answers Content Extraction : Pull out key information, data points, and insights Multi-format Support : Work with PDF, Word documents,

entry.title: Web search
entry.content: A new web search API is now available in Ollama. Ollama provides a generous free tier of web searches for individuals to use, and higher rate limits are available via Ollama’s cloud.

entry.title: New model scheduling
entry.content: Ollama now includes a significantly improved model scheduling system, reducing crashes due to out of memory issues, maximizing GPU utilization and performance, especially on multi-GPU systems.

entry.title: Cloud models
entry.content: Cloud models are now in preview, letting you run larger models with fast, datacenter-grade hardware. You can keep using your local tools while running larger models that wouldn’t fit on a personal computer.

entry.title: Defeating Nondeterminism in LLM Inference
entry.content: Reproducibility is a bedrock of scientific progress. However, it’s remarkably difficult to get reproducible results out of large language models. For example, you might observe that asking ChatGPT the same question multiple times provides different results. This by itself is not surprising, since getting a result from a language model involves “sampling”, a process that converts the language model’s output into a probability distribution and probabilistically selects a token. What might be more

entry.title: Mistral AI raises 1.7B€ to accelerate technological progress with AI
entry.content: Mistral AI raises 1.7B€ to accelerate technological progress with AI

entry.title: Make Memory work for you.
entry.content: Make Memory work for you.

entry.title: Le Chat. Custom MCP connectors. Memories.
entry.content: Le Chat. Custom MCP connectors. Memories.

entry.title: August 2025
entry.content: API model deprecation notice Please note that as of August 1, 2025, R1-1776 will be removed from the available models. R1 has been a popular option for a while, but it hasn't kept pace with recent improvements and lacks support for newer features. To reduce engineering overhead and make room for more capable models, we're retiring it from the API. If you liked R1's strengths, we recommend switching to Sonar Pro Reasoning . It offers similar behavior with stronger overall performance.

entry.title: OpenAI gpt-oss
entry.content: Ollama partners with OpenAI to bring gpt-oss to Ollama and its community.

entry.title: Unlocking the potential of vision language models on satellite imagery through fine-tuning
entry.content: Unlocking the potential of vision language models on satellite imagery through fine-tuning

entry.title: July 2025
entry.content: New: Detailed Cost Information in API Responses The API response JSON now includes detailed cost information for each request. You'll now see a new structure like this in your response: What's included: input_tokens_cost : Cost attributed to input tokens output_tokens_cost : Cost attributed to output tokens request_cost : Fixed cost per request total_cost : The total cost for this API call This update enables easier tracking of usage and billing directly from each API response, giving you comple

entry.title: Announcing Codestral 25.08 and the Complete Mistral Coding Stack for Enterprise
entry.content: Announcing Codestral 25.08 and the Complete Mistral Coding Stack for Enterprise

entry.title: Ollama's new app
entry.content: Ollama's new app is now available for macOS and Windows.

entry.title: Our contribution to a global environmental standard for AI
entry.content: Our contribution to a global environmental standard for AI

entry.title: June 2025
entry.content: Enhanced: Date Range Filtering with Latest Updated Field We've enhanced our date range filtering capabilities with new fields that give you even more control over search results based on content freshness and updates. New fields available: latest_updated : Filter results based on when the webpage was last modified or updated published_after : Filter by original publication date (existing) published_before : Filter by original publication date (existing) The latest_updated field is particularly u

entry.title: Le Chat dives deep.
entry.content: Le Chat dives deep.

entry.title: Voxtral
entry.content: Voxtral

entry.title: Upgrading agentic coding capabilities with the new Devstral models
entry.content: Upgrading agentic coding capabilities with the new Devstral models

entry.title: Announcing AI for Citizens
entry.content: Announcing AI for Citizens

entry.title: Mistral Compute
entry.content: Mistral Compute

entry.title: Magistral
entry.content: Magistral

entry.title: Introducing Mistral Code
entry.content: Introducing Mistral Code

entry.title: Secure Minions: private collaboration between Ollama and frontier models
entry.content: Secure Minions is a secure protocol built by Stanford's Hazy Research lab to allow encrypted local-remote communication.

entry.title: Thinking
entry.content: Ollama now has the ability to enable or disable thinking. This gives users the flexibility to choose the model’s thinking behavior for different applications and use cases.

entry.title: Codestral Embed
entry.content: Codestral Embed

entry.title: Streaming responses with tool calling
entry.content: Ollama now supports streaming responses with tool calling. This enables all chat applications to stream content and also call tools in real time.

entry.title: Build AI agents with the Mistral Agents API
entry.content: Build AI agents with the Mistral Agents API

entry.title: Devstral
entry.content: Devstral

entry.title: Ollama's new engine for multimodal models
entry.content: Ollama now supports new multimodal models with its new engine.

entry.title: Introducing Le Chat Enterprise
entry.content: Introducing Le Chat Enterprise

entry.title: Minions: where local and cloud LLMs meet
entry.content: Avanika Narayan, Dan Biderman, and Sabri Eyuboglu from Christopher Ré's Stanford Hazy Research lab, along with Avner May, Scott Linderman, James Zou, have developed a way to shift a substantial portion of LLM workloads to consumer devices by having small on-device models (such as Llama 3.2 with Ollama) collaborate with larger models in the cloud (such as GPT-4o).

entry.title: Structured outputs
entry.content: Ollama now supports structured outputs making it possible to constrain a model's output to a specific format defined by a JSON schema. The Ollama Python and JavaScript libraries have been updated to support structured outputs.

entry.title: Ollama Python library 0.4 with function calling improvements
entry.content: With Ollama Python library version 0.4, functions can now be provided as tools. The library now also has full typing support and new examples have been added.

entry.title: Llama 3.2 Vision
entry.content: Llama 3.2 Vision 11B and 90B models are now available in Ollama.

entry.title: IBM Granite 3.0 models
entry.content: Ollama partners with IBM to bring Granite 3.0 models to Ollama.

entry.title: Llama 3.2 goes small and multimodal
entry.content: Ollama partners with Meta to bring Llama 3.2 to Ollama.

entry.title: Reduce hallucinations with Bespoke-Minicheck
entry.content: Bespoke-Minicheck is a new grounded factuality checking model developed by Bespoke Labs that is now available in Ollama. It can fact-check responses generated by other models to detect and reduce hallucinations.

entry.title: Tool support
entry.content: Ollama now supports tool calling with popular models such as Llama 3.1. This enables a model to answer a given prompt using tool(s) it knows about, making it possible for models to perform more complex tasks or interact with the outside world.

entry.title: Google Gemma 2
entry.content: Gemma 2 is now available on Ollama in 3 sizes - 2B, 9B and 27B.

entry.title: An entirely open-source AI code assistant inside your editor
entry.content: Continue enables you to easily create your own coding assistant directly inside Visual Studio Code and JetBrains with open-source LLMs.

entry.title: Google announces Firebase Genkit with Ollama support
entry.content: At Google IO 2024, Google announced Ollama support in Firebase Genkit, a new open-source framework for developers to build, deploy and monitor production-ready AI-powered apps.

entry.title: Llama 3 is not very censored
entry.content: Compared to Llama 2, Llama 3 feels much less censored. Meta has substantially lowered false refusal rates. Llama 3 will refuse less than 1/3 of the prompts previously refused by Llama 2.

entry.title: Llama 3
entry.content: Llama 3 is now available to run on Ollama. This model is the next generation of Meta's state-of-the-art large language model, and is the most capable openly available LLM to date.

entry.title: Embedding models
entry.content: Embedding models are available in Ollama, making it easy to generate vector embeddings for use in search and retrieval augmented generation (RAG) applications.
